Performing initial audit of PKGBUILD to ensure that we can safely run `makepkg`
LLM auditresponse for PKGBUILD:
<security_assessment>
<decision>SAFE</decision>
<details>
The PKGBUILD defines a standard VCS-based AUR package for `paper-gtk-theme-git`. The `source` array correctly points to the official GitHub repository via `git+https`, which is expected and safe. The `sha512sums` entry is set to `SKIP`, which is standard and required for git sources — it does not pose a risk in this context.

Execution during `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` includes sourcing the PKGBUILD and running `pkgver()`. The `pkgver()` function updates the version using `git rev-list` and `git rev-parse` on the already-cloned repository, which is safe and commonly used in `-git` packages. No network requests, data exfiltration, or code execution from external sources occurs at this stage beyond the initial clone, which is part of normal AUR behavior.

There are no signs of malicious logic in the global scope or in `pkgver()`, such as `curl`/`wget` inside command substitution, `eval` of remote content, or obfuscated strings. The commands run during this phase are limited to version computation from the local git repository.
</details>
<summary>
Safe: normal VCS package, no risky code in sourcing
</summary>
</security_assessment>

+ Initial PKGBUILD audit complete [grey50]-- Safe: normal VCS package, no risky code in sourcing[/]

<details>
The PKGBUILD declares a standard git source from the official GitHub repository of the `paper-gtk-theme` project (`git+https://github.com/snwh/paper-gtk-theme.git`). The `pkgver()` function only performs local git operations (`git rev-list` and `git rev-parse`) inside the cloned source directory to derive a version string. No top-level code, `eval`, network requests to unexpected hosts, or other dangerous commands are present. The scope of `makepkg --nobuild --nodeps --noprepare` and `makepkg --printsrcinfo` is limited to sourcing the PKGBUILD and running `pkgver()`, neither of which execute any malicious or suspicious payload.
</details>
<summary>
